baseline.worktree_note: The merge commit is the exact behavioral baseline. During evidence capture the only worktree overlay was this new documentation assessment package; all non-doc paths matched the commit.

baseline_transition.scope: TS-02 source completion atomicity and exact pre-fix image reconciliation

environment.dotenv: present, but pytest-dotenv was explicitly disabled for EV-001 through EV-004; values not loaded or captured
environment.database_profile: SQLite via repository and E2E recovery fixtures

structure_snapshot.limitation: The index is fresh at the merge commit, but doctor reports classifier-tag failures and eight stale file entities. This assessment makes no unreachability claim from graph absence.

tracker_snapshot.limitation: Tracker state is a non-atomic mutable snapshot and is ownership evidence, never behavioral proof.

evidence.warning_note: Disabling pytest-dotenv leaves the configured env_files option unknown; pytest emits one PytestConfigWarning. No .env values are loaded.
evidence.establishes: Narrow SQLite repository and executable source-contract evidence for strict authority, stale-token zero-mutation refusal, legacy-adapter isolation, and pending-sink bundle admission/refusal.
evidence.does_not_establish: Production orchestration, independent-process contention, expiry/reclaim, every subtype, plugin composition, or crash/restart convergence.

evidence.warning_note: Disabling pytest-dotenv leaves the configured env_files option unknown; pytest emits one PytestConfigWarning. No .env values are loaded.
evidence.establishes: Direct barrier snapshot/refusal/rollback evidence and built-in local sink response-loss, diversion, member-classification, and virtual-effect pipeline evidence.
evidence.does_not_establish: Abrupt process death, external sinks, long effect calls, generation takeover, every sink seam, aggregation continuation, or complete plugin lifecycle behavior.

evidence.warning_note: Disabling pytest-dotenv leaves the configured env_files option unknown; pytest emits one PytestConfigWarning. No .env values are loaded.
evidence.establishes: Direct SQLite repository evidence for exact TS-07 through TS-10 success images, subtype/owner/membership/bundle refusals, and event/branch-loss rollback.
evidence.does_not_establish: Production plugin/follower composition, contention, crash/restart, every database profile, lifecycle, or read-model truth tables.

evidence.warning_note: Disabling pytest-dotenv leaves the configured env_files option unknown; pytest emits one PytestConfigWarning. No .env values are loaded.
evidence.establishes: Current production process_row commits row, token, source COMPLETED witness, initial work/lease, and scheduler events in one transaction; injected scheduler failure rolls back the asserted row, token, source-state, and work-item planes. The compatibility reconciler accepts only the exact pre-fix attempt-1 witness, rejects duplicate, mismatched, malformed, later-attempt, or conflicting evidence without synthesizing state, and a crash-after-repair public resume invokes the transform exactly once.
evidence.does_not_establish: Abrupt operating-system process death, independent-process contention, every source exclusion/failure/quarantine arm, non-SQLite profiles, or all ten TS-02/PB-01 dimensions.

legs.reason: Source inspection found that a losing seat CAS can still depart membership and that the membership update is not run-scoped.

legs.reason: Source failure/quarantine scheduler exclusion is not complete production evidence.
legs.exit_gate: Every pre-row failure and exclusion path records audit evidence and creates no scheduler row.

legs.reason: Deleted WAITING state lacks one maintained reachability/refusal regression package.
legs.exit_gate: Vocabulary, schema, writers, readers, restore paths, and production config prove absence and refusal.

hard_gates.reason: Pending-sink malformed bundles are covered narrowly; admission and recovery of every durable subtype are not.
Strict helper/source-contract evidence is strong, but every live leader-seat and production write path is not covered.
The required independent-process queue, coordination, and effect contention matrices are incomplete.
Selected refusal images pass; the named repository-wide durable-plane cases do not.
Current TS-02 source completion, barrier completion, and dispositions have narrow atomicity evidence; TS-18 and the full run-coordination state/evidence matrix remain unresolved.

The named pre-fix source-completion image converges under deterministic public-resume crash injection, but abrupt-process and the remaining scheduler, plugin, barrier, and sink restart seams remain unproved.
All scheduler, coordination, resume, redrive, and barrier-intake read models lack complete production truth-table evidence; queue-only hold reachability is unresolved.
Fresh, resume, follower, partial-start, plugin boundary, and teardown coverage is incomplete.
All 68 legs contain at least one unknown or partial mandatory cell; this is a global gate and does not by itself convert every leg from unknown to gap.
RC-04 and RC-07 repository predicates are weaker than their required fail-closed contracts; the architecture and assessment now expose rather than conceal that source/contract mismatch.

derived.reason: The source-completion seam is fixed and narrowly evidenced, but every leg still has at least one unresolved mandatory cell and every repository-wide hard gate remains open.

limitations: Evidence remains deliberately conservative: prior passing runs and closed issues were not promoted without fresh exact attachment.
The focused runs use the repository SQLite test profile and do not establish every advertised database or deployment profile.
The source crash/restart evidence uses deterministic BaseException injection and fresh public-resume objects, not abrupt operating-system process death.
Loomweave is fresh at the commit but reports classifier-tag failures and eight stale file entities, so graph absence is not an unreachability proof.
The tracker snapshot is mutable ownership context and was not used as behavioral proof.
